response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's background is primarily TypeScript/Node.js full-stack web development with no meaningful C++ experience — C++ isn't even listed among his skills, and the only C/C++ reference is a bachelor thesis project and roughly six months at National Instruments. The role requires 8 years of C++ programming as a minimum qualification, which alone disqualifies the candidate. Beyond the language gap, there is no demonstrated experience in cloud compute platform architecture, no formal technical leadership track record at the required scale, and no evidence of the infrastructure depth (compute, storage, hardware architecture) central to this role.
strengths: Broad exposure to distributed systems and high-throughput services (millions of HTTP requests/second at Dunnhumby)
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code quality mentoring via workshops and dojos
Hands-on infrastructure and DevOps experience: Kubernetes deployments, AWS CDK, GitLab CI, Docker, containerised services
concerns: C++ is entirely absent from the skills list; the role requires 8 years of C++ as a hard minimum qualification
No cloud compute platform or systems-level infrastructure experience — work is application-layer (web, mobile, direct mail, GIS)
No formal technical leadership role: staff/senior IC titles throughout, with no evidence of owning large-scale platform strategy or managing cross-team technical alignment

key_factors.factor: No cloud compute / systems infrastructure depth
